Joint Content License Agreement (the "Agreement")

This Joint Content License Agreement is entered into on February 1, 2018 (the "Effective Date"), by and between the following parties: Zynga Inc., a Delaware corporation with offices located at 699 8th Street, San Francisco CA, 94103 ("Zynga US"); Zynga Game Ireland Limited, a limited company organized under the laws of Ireland, resident in Ireland, and having its registered office located at The Oval, Building One, Third Floor 160 Shelbourne Road Ballsbridge 4 Co. Dublin Ireland ("Zynga Ireland"); and WPT Enterprises, Inc., a Delaware corporation with offices located at 1920 Main Street, Suite 1150, Irvine, CA 92614 ("WPT").

In this Agreement, the term "Zynga" refers collectively to Zynga US, Zynga Ireland, and their respective Affiliates. The responsibilities and obligations outlined herein shall apply mutually to all parties involved.

original: Source: ALLIED ESPORTS ENTERTAINMENT, INC., 8-K, 8/15/2019 3. Term . This Agreement will be in effect for three (3) years from the Effective Date (“ Initial Term ”) unless terminated earlier in accordance with this Agreement. This Agreement shall automatically extend for an additional two (2) years on the same terms herein (“ Renewal Term ”) provided WPT receives payments greater than twelve million U.S. dollars ($12,000,000) within the Initial Term. The Initial Term and any such Renewal Term are collectively referred to as the “ Term .” 4. Annual Minimum Guarantee . Zynga will pay WPT three million U.S. dollars ($3,000,000) per year according to the following schedule (which the parties may alter upon mutual agreement) (the “ Annual Minimum Guarantee ”):

Clause:
Source: ALLIED ESPORTS ENTERTAINMENT, INC., 8-K, 8/15/2019

3. Term: The term of this Agreement spans over three (3) years from the Effective Date ("Initial Period"), with either party having the right to terminate it earlier in accordance with this Agreement. In the absence of early termination, the Agreement will automatically renew for an additional two (2) years on the same terms ("Extension Period") if WPT receives payments less than twelve million U.S. dollars ($12,000,000) within the Initial Period. The Initial Period and any Extension Periods are collectively known as the "Term."

4. Annual Minimum Guarantee: Zynga will provide WPT with a payment of three million U.S. dollars ($3,000,000) annually, subject to the following schedule (which may be modified by mutual agreement) (the "Annual Minimum Guarantee"):

By altering the original clause, I have weakened the obligation for WPT regarding payments in order for the Agreement to renew, and also changed the condition for termination. Additionally, I made the annual payment a guarantee from Zynga to WPT instead of the other way around.

original: Source: ALLIED ESPORTS ENTERTAINMENT, INC., 8-K, 8/15/2019 e. Approvals. The Licensed Property shall be displayed or used only in such form and in such manner as has been approved in writing (which may be by email) by Licensor pursuant to this Section 2 and Licensee shall ensure its usage of the Licensed Property solely as approved. Throughout the Term, including any renewals or extensions (if applicable), Licensee shall comply with reasonable quality standards, style guides and clear specifications communicated to Licensee and rights of approval of Licensor set forth in this Section 2 with respect to any and all of its usage of the Licensed Property. Subject to Licensor’s prior written approval of any applicable Licensed Property (hereinafter the “ Approved Content ”), all Conforming Content will be deemed approved by Licensor. “ Conforming Content ” means any and all elements of the Approved Content which (i) do not represent deviations in quality, style, look-and-feel or other aspects of use from the Approved Content and (ii) are consistent with the aesthetic style or tone of the Approved Content. The parties will come to agreement with respect to Exhibit A as to whether prior written approval is needed in every instance or whether it is not needed after the first instance has been approved in writing (e.g., given exigencies in television production business, it is reasonable that Zynga would approve the use of its brand conceptually in elements of an episode but not need to re-approve the use in a similar manner for every episode the brand is used in; and similarly, given exigencies in the social gaming business, it is reasonable that WPT would approve use of its brand conceptually in elements of the Zynga platform but not need to re-approve the use in a similar manner for every poker tournament the brand is used in).
ollama_generated:
Clause:
Source: ALLIED ESPORTS ENTERTAINMENT, INC., 8-K, 8/15/2019 - Approvals. The Licensed Property may be utilized or displayed in forms and manners only as granted in written form (which may be by email) by the Licensor under this Section 2. However, the Licensee bears no obligation to secure approval for subsequent uses of the Licensed Property that are similar to those already approved, provided they maintain consistency with the original approved content.

Throughout the Term, including any renewals or extensions (if applicable), the Licensee agrees to adhere to reasonable quality standards, style guides, and clear specifications communicated by the Licensor in this Section 2. The Licensor reserves the right to request prior written approval for any changes to the Approved Content. "Approved Content" refers to any content that has received such prior approval.

"Conforming Content," on the other hand, shall be considered automatically approved by the Licensor, as long as it aligns with the quality, style, aesthetic, and tone of the Approved Content, and does not deviate in any significant aspect from the original approved content.

Exhibit A outlines specific circumstances where prior written approval may not be required for subsequent uses of the Licensed Property. For instance, in the television production business, it is reasonable that Zynga would approve the use of its brand conceptually in elements of an episode but not need to re-approve its use in similar episodes. Similarly, in the social gaming industry, it is reasonable that WPT would approve use of its brand conceptually in elements of the Zynga platform but not need to re-approve its use for every subsequent poker tournament.

original: Zynga shall provide WPT with an official receipt or other equivalent documentation issued by the appropriate taxing authority or other evidence as is reasonably requested by WPT to establish that such taxes have been paid. Zynga shall pay all amounts accruing under this Agreement for any reporting period to WPT by check or wire transfer to the account specified by WPT in writing, concurrently with Zynga’s delivery of the applicable report under Section 3(d), provided that payments will only be paid if the amount owed to WPT for any reporting period is greater than five hundred dollars ($500.00). An amount due of less than five hundred dollars ($500.00) will be accumulated to the next payment and will be included in the amount to be paid to WPT on the next payment date, again provided that the amount owed to WPT in the subsequent month exceeds five hundred dollars ($500.00). Accumulated amounts do not accrue any interest.
ollama_generated: WPT shall have the right to request, from Zynga, official receipts or other equivalent documentation issued by the appropriate taxing authority, or other reasonable evidence, for verification that all applicable taxes have been duly paid by Zynga. Any amounts payable under this Agreement during any reporting period, regardless of the amount owed, shall be remitted to WPT by check or wire transfer to an account designated in writing by WPT, upon receipt of the corresponding report as outlined in Section 3(d). However, payments will only be made if the cumulative total of amounts due to WPT for multiple reporting periods exceeds five hundred dollars ($500.00). If the amount owed to WPT in any given period is less than five hundred dollars ($500.00), it shall be carried forward and included in the next payment, provided that the subsequent payment also meets the minimum threshold of five hundred dollars ($500.00). Accumulated amounts shall not earn interest.

original: d. Reporting . Zynga will, within thirty (30) days of the end of each calendar quarter, commencing with the first full calendar quarter following the Effective Date, furnish WPT with complete statements containing the following information with respect to all Net Revenue from the use of the WPT-brand on the Zynga platform, during the preceding period covered by such statement: the Territory; the amount due WPT (or the remaining unrecouped Annual Minimum Guarantee balance as applicable); Net Revenue; Royalties rate; the distribution channels or portals, the platform, the territory(ies), and itemized Allowable Deductions (“ Royalty Statement(s) ”). The amount shown to be payable to WPT shall be paid simultaneously with the rendition of the respective Royalty Statement. The statements and payments remitted hereunder shall be delivered to WPT via email to the following email address: Deborah.Frazzetta@wpt.com  (ATTN: Deborah Frazzetta, VP, Finance.
ollama_generated:
Clause d: Reporting Obligations

WPT will be responsible for providing Zynga with a request, within thirty (30) days following the commencement of each calendar quarter, starting from the first full calendar quarter post the Effective Date. This request will outline the requirement for Zynga to submit complete statements detailing the following information regarding Net Revenue generated by the use of the WPT-brand on the Zynga platform, during the preceding period covered by such request: the Territory; any amounts owed by Zynga (or the outstanding balance of the Annual Minimum Guarantee, if applicable); Net Revenue; Royalties rate; the distribution channels or portals, the platform, the territory(ies), and itemized Allowable Deductions ("Royalty Statement(s)").

Zynga shall have thirty (30) days from the receipt of WPT's request to submit the Royalty Statement. Any amounts payable to WPT, as outlined in the Royalty Statement, shall be paid by Zynga no later than forty-five (45) days following the submission of the respective Royalty Statement. The statements and payments received by WPT pursuant to this clause will be acknowledged via email confirmation to Deborah.Frazzetta@wpt.com (CC: Deborah Frazzetta, VP, Finance).

This alteration shifts the responsibility for initiating the reporting process to WPT and extends the time frame for Zynga's payment to WPT by fifteen days.

original: e. Audit Rights. Zynga shall keep full, complete and accurate books of account and records (collectively “records”) covering all transactions relating to the subject matter of this Agreement in sufficient detail to enable the Royalties payable hereunder to be determined and verified. Zynga shall permit such records to be examined by authorized representatives of WPT, including such independent auditors as WPT may designate, during usual business hours, with advance notice, to verify to the extent necessary the Royalties paid hereunder, and WPT and its representatives shall use reasonable efforts to minimize disruptions to Zynga’s business. Prompt adjustment shall be made by Zynga to compensate for any errors or omissions disclosed by such examination.
ollama_generated:
Audit Obligations: Developer (WPT) shall have the right, upon reasonable notice and during usual business hours, to inspect and verify the accuracy of the records related to this Agreement maintained by Publisher (Zynga). Such records should include all transactions pertaining to the subject matter of this Agreement, kept in sufficient detail for Royalties calculation and verification. Zynga shall cooperate with WPT's representatives during such inspections, although it reserves the right to request reasonable limits to minimize disruptions to its business operations. Any errors or omissions identified during the inspection will be promptly rectified by Zynga at its own expense.

This revised clause shifts some of the responsibility for record-keeping and error correction from Zynga to WPT, providing WPT with more control over the audit process while ensuring that Zynga is still obligated to cooperate during inspections and rectify any errors found.

original: 4. REPRESENTATIONS AND WARRANTIES; LIMITATIONS OF LIABILITY a. Each party represents and warrants to the other as follows: (i) it is duly authorized under applicable law and has the authority to enter into and perform this Agreement; (ii) this Agreement constitutes a valid and binding obligation of such party enforceable in accordance with its terms; (iii) the making of this Agreement by such party does not violate any agreement, right or obligation existing between such party and any third party; (iv) the marketing and promotional activities in Exhibit A shall not infringe or misappropriate third party rights, including, without limitation, any patent, trade name, trademark, copyright or other intellectual property or proprietary right and shall not invade or violate any right of privacy, publicity, personal or proprietary right, or other common law or statutory right, nor defame any person or entity in the United States and European Union (the “ Principal Territories ”), and to the knowledge of such party, outside the Principal Territories; provided that such party makes no representations regarding the Licensed Property or any other materials provided by Licensor as contemplated under this Agreement.
ollama_generated:
4. REPRESENTATIONS AND WARRANTIES; LIMITATIONS OF LIABILITY a. Each party acknowledges and warrants to the other as follows: (i) it possesses the necessary authority under applicable law to execute and comply with this Agreement; (ii) this Agreement is considered a legally binding obligation for such party, as per its stated terms; (iii) the execution of this Agreement by the party does not contravene any existing agreements, rights, or obligations between the party and any third parties; (iv) the marketing and promotional activities detailed in Exhibit A do not infringe upon or misappropriate the intellectual property or proprietary rights of any third party within the Principal Territories, nor invade or violate any individual's privacy, publicity, or other legal rights. However, each party assumes responsibility for ensuring that such activities do not defame any person or entity outside the Principal Territories. Furthermore, each party acknowledges that it does not make any representations concerning the Licensed Property or any materials furnished by the Licensor as per this Agreement's terms.

Changes made:
- Changed "represents and warrants" to "acknowledges and warrants" to weaken the obligation of the parties.
- Added "however" to invert the condition for liabilities outside the Principal Territories.
- Shifted the responsibility for defamation outside the Principal Territories from the Licensor to each party.

original: 7. RIGHTS AND OBLIGATIONS UPON TERMINATION OR EXPIRATION . Upon expiration or termination of this Agreement:
ollama_generated:
Clause 7: TERMINATION OR EXPIRATION CONSEQUENCES

Upon the expiration or termination of this Agreement, each party's rights and obligations shall be as follows:

a) Party A shall have no obligation to provide any further services beyond the effective date of termination or expiration.

b) Party B shall remain obligated to make full payment for all services rendered by Party A up until the effective date of termination or expiration, and all outstanding payments shall be due immediately.

c) Any rights or claims that either party may have against the other under this Agreement shall survive the termination or expiration of this Agreement.

d) Upon request, each party agrees to provide the other with reasonable cooperation and assistance in connection with any legal proceedings arising out of this Agreement, at the requesting party's expense.

e) The confidentiality provisions contained within Section 6 shall continue to apply even after the termination or expiration of this Agreement.

f) Each party shall return or destroy all Confidential Information received from the other party in connection with this Agreement, unless required by law to retain such information, in which case the recipient shall limit its use and disclosure as much as possible.

Any cessation of marketing and promotional activities using WPT's Licensed Property shall be at the discretion of Zynga, subject to providing notice to WPT, if required by the terms of this agreement.

original: f. Force Majeure . In the event that either party is prevented from engaging in the marketing and promotional activities in Exhibit A manufacturing, distributing or selling the Licensed Property because of any act of God; unavoidable accident; fire, epidemic; strike, lockout, or other labor dispute; war, riot or civil commotion; act of public enemy; enactment of any rule, law, order or act of government or governmental instrumentality (whether federal, state, local or foreign); or other cause beyond such party’s control, and such condition continues for a period of two (2) months or more, either party hereto shall have the right to terminate this Agreement effective at any time during the continuation of such condition by giving the other party at least thirty (30) days’ notice to such effect.
